Distribution & relationship plots
5 panels
Genome-wide H distributionS1 · S2
226 samples, mean = 4.55 × 10⁻³, range 2.83 × 10⁻³ to 7.08 × 10⁻³.
Genome-wide F_ROH distributionS1 · S2
226 samples, mean = 0.277, IQR 0.250–0.308, max = 0.407.
H × F_ROH (Spearman ρ = −0.704)S1 · S3
Per-individual scatter, dots coloured by K=8 ancestry. The two estimators agree strongly: P = 3.4 × 10⁻³⁵.
H × longest ROH (ρ = −0.242)S1 · S3
Weaker than total F_ROH: longest single tract carries different information.
θ inside vs outside ROHS1 · S12
QC for ROH calls — typical ratio 0.20–0.30 (4–5× depression inside ROH). High ratios may flag false-positive tracts.
Per-sample θπ summary (ST2)ST2
Per-sample mean θπ across 1,895 windows. Cohort tightly clustered around 4.5 × 10⁻³.
Per-chromosome diversity
28 linkage groups × θπ (ST1) and F_ROH (S4). Plus per-chromosome ancestry KW tests (S6).
ST1 · Per-chromosome θπ (500 kb non-overlapping)
28 rows · 1,895 windows
chromosome n windows mean θπ median IQR Q25 Q75 min max
S4 · Per-chromosome F_ROH
28 rows

A small number of individuals carry chromosome-level F_ROH > 1 (max reaches ~1.4 on LG15). This reflects ROH tracts extending past the per-chromosome callable-mask denominator — not biologically impossible autozygosity, and does not affect rank-based tests.

chromosome n samples mean F_ROH median SD min max
S6 · Per-chromosome F_ROH × K=8 ancestry KW tests (all226)
28 rows · BH-adjusted

Per-chromosome Kruskal–Wallis F_ROH × K=8 ancestry test. n varies because individuals with zero callable sites on a given chromosome are dropped. Significance: *** P ≤ 0.001, ** P ≤ 0.01, * P ≤ 0.05, all BH-adjusted across 28 chromosomes.

chromosome n median F_ROH IQR KW H P (raw) P (BH) sig.

4 panels
Mean θπ per chromosome (ranked)ST1
Bars: cohort-mean θπ; whiskers: Q25–Q75. Genome mean dashed reference.
Mean F_ROH per chromosome (ranked)S4
Bars: per-chr mean F_ROH; error bars: ±SD across 222–225 samples.
−log₁₀(P_BH) — F_ROH × K=8 per-chrS6
Per-chromosome Kruskal–Wallis P, BH-adjusted across 28 chromosomes. Reference lines at α = 0.05, 0.01, 0.001.
ngsF-HMM convergence stabilitySZ
Per-chromosome 'Gap best–worst' across 20 random-seed replicates. Lower = more stable. Ribbon: Gap best–5th.
θπ hotspots
19 outlier windows above the 99th percentile (mean θπ > 5.27 × 10⁻³). Strongest peak: LG22 19.0–19.5 Mb at 1.07 × 10⁻² (2.4× genome mean).
Genome-wide θπ landscape
ST1 + ST3
Per-chromosome mean θπ shown as horizontal bars (length = chromosome length scaled, height = mean θπ at chrom scale); outlier 500 kb windows from ST3 plotted as red ticks at their genomic position. Zoomable horizontal layout — click a chromosome to filter the table.
ST3 · 19 outlier windows (mean θπ > 99th percentile)
19 rows

Adjacent-window groups (within 1 Mb on the same chromosome) are flagged as one cluster — they likely reflect single localised diversity peaks spanning 1–4 Mb, not independent loci. LG12 4.5–8.5 Mb is one cluster of 9 windows, not 9 hits.

chr start (Mb) end (Mb) mean θπ × genome mean percentile cluster
ST3b · Per-sample θπ heatmap at the 19 hotspots
226 × 19 matrix

Rows: 226 samples (sorted by mean θπ across these 19 windows, descending). Columns: 19 outlier windows in ST3 column order. Hue: per-sample θπ value. Hover any cell to read the exact value and sample. Reveals which samples drive each hotspot — wide variation across samples at one window suggests a polymorphic signal (candidate inversion); uniform colour suggests a fixed feature.

Ancestry × diversity
K=8 NGSadmix structure × H and F_ROH. Plus K=2–12 sweep, per-Q correlations, BH-adjusted pairwise contrasts.
S5 · The all226 → pruned81 collapse
central finding

F_ROH × ancestry differs significantly in the full 226-sample panel (KW P = 2.9 × 10⁻⁴) but the signal collapses to non-significance in pruned81 (P = 0.59). H × ancestry is non-significant in either panel. Interpretation: covariance reflects recent shared inbreeding among siblings within ancestry components, not divergence among founder lineages.

test variable panel n statistic H P-value
S5 · Significant pairwise Wilcoxon F_ROH contrasts (all226, BH-adjusted)
5 contrasts

All five significant contrasts involve K5 — but K5 has the lowest median F_ROH (0.247), not the highest. K5 is the cluster being depressed below K1, K2, K6, K7, K8.

contrastP (BH-adj.)

S10 · K=8 Q-component × H correlations
8 rows

No Q-component shows a significant Pearson or Spearman correlation with per-individual H after BH adjustment — consistent with the non-significant overall KW H × ancestry test (S5).

component panel n Pearson r Pearson P P (BH) Spearman ρ Spearman P P (BH)

Box: Q25–Q75. Whisker: P5–P95. Stripe: cohort mean. K5 (green) carries the lowest median.

Boxes essentially overlapping — KW P = 0.150, no ancestry-level H differentiation.

methods
S8 · Per-sample F_ROH by length class (long format)
1,130 rows

For each (sample × length class), tract count and total bp inside ROH of that class. Visualised as stacked composition bars below.

sample length class n tracts ROH (bp) ROH (Mb) % callable
ROH composition plots
3 panels
Cohort ROH length spectrumS8
Aggregated tract count + total Mb per length class across the cohort. Long tracts (> 5 Mb) are recent inbreeding signal; short tracts (< 1 Mb) are ancient bottleneck.
Per-sample stacked F_ROH compositionS8
Stacked bars: composition of total F_ROH across length classes. Sorted by total F_ROH desc; legend by class. Shows the qualitative shift from short-dominated (ancient inbreeding) to long-dominated (recent) as F_ROH increases.
top 60 + bottom 60 all 226 top 100
S8b · Per-chromosome × per-sample F_ROH heatmapS8b
Rows: 226 samples (sorted by total F_ROH descending). Columns: 28 chromosomes. Hue: F_ROH on that chromosome (capped at 1.0; cells > 1 are flagged from per-chr callable-mask shortfalls). Hover for sample × chrom F_ROH.
S12 · Heterozygosity inside vs outside ROH (per sample)
226 rows · derived from S1 θ_in / θ_out

Quantifies the diversity contrast between inbred (inside ROH) and non-inbred (outside ROH) genomic segments. A high het_ratio_out_over_in confirms the ROH calls are real (true ROH should have very low internal heterozygosity). Cohort median ratio: —; outliers flagged where ratio ≤ Q05 (—) — these are candidates for ngsF-HMM mis-calls or coverage artefact.

all 226 outliers only
sample callable bp in ROH callable bp out ROH het in ROH het out ROH ratio out/in outlier?
S8c · Long-ROH tract atlas (≥ 1 Mb)
5,850 of 681,286 tracts

Individual ROH tracts (the manuscript-relevant subset, length ≥ 1 Mb). Each row is one ROH segment in one sample. The full S8c table contains 681,286 rows — embedding all of them would require ~28 MB of JSON, so this view shows the long-tract subset that drives the F_ROH calculations (≥ 1 Mb is the ngsF-HMM detection threshold for ~9× coverage). Filter by sample, chromosome, or length to drill into specific founder events.

sample chr start (Mb) end (Mb) length (Mb)
Pruning & QC
Cross-validation and quality controls: NAToRA pruning (S11), ngsF-HMM convergence (SZ), Spearman correlation matrix (S3), het in/out ROH (S12 / S1).
S11 · First-degree kinship pruning (NAToRA, θ ≥ 0.177)
226 rows · 81 retained

Graph-based first-degree kinship pruning yields a maximum independent set of 81 samples. The ancestry × F_ROH signal in all226 (S5, P = 2.9 × 10⁻⁴) collapses in the pruned81 panel (P = 0.59) — confirming the signal is family structure within ancestry clusters, not founder-lineage divergence.

all 226 retained (81) removed (145)
sample status K=8 H F_HOM F_ROH
SZ · ngsF-HMM convergence per chromosome
28 rows · 20 seeds each

20 random-seed replicates per chromosome (seeds 32–51); the highest log-likelihood replicate is retained. 'Gap best–worst' = absolute log-likelihood difference between best and worst seed. Lower = more consistent convergence. 26/28 chromosomes labelled 'very stable'.

chromosome reps best rep best log-lik gap best–worst gap best–5th stability
S3 · Spearman correlations between H-based and ROH-based estimators
4 rows

H × F_ROH (ρ = −0.704) and F_HOM × F_ROH (ρ = +0.704) have equal magnitude because F_HOM is a linear transform of H. This equivalence is an HMM-free cross-validation of the ngsF-HMM ROH calls.

variable 1 variable 2 n Spearman ρ P-value direction
QC plots
3 panels
F_ROH: pruned81 vs. removedS11
Side-by-side box of F_ROH within retained vs. removed samples. The pruning step does not select for low-F_ROH individuals; both groups span similar F_ROH.
ngsF-HMM stability rankedSZ
28 chromosomes ranked by best–worst log-lik gap (smaller = more stable). Two outlier chromosomes flagged for higher seed sensitivity.
Het in vs. out ROH ratio (cohort)S1 · S12
Per-sample ratio of θ inside ROH / θ outside ROH. Distribution centered around 0.20–0.30 (4–5× depression inside ROH); high outliers may flag false-positive tracts.
About this atlas
Scope, four-atlas family, headline numbers, MODULE_3 methods documentation, glossary, lineage.
What this atlas is
scope

The Diversity Atlas v2 is the population-genetics-reviewer-facing view of the 226-sample C. gariepinus hatchery cohort — a deep per-sample and per-chromosome lens on nucleotide diversity, autozygosity (F_ROH), ROH composition, and ancestry-stratified tests. Backing data is the MODULE_3 supplementary table package (MODULE3_Supplementary_Tables_7) — all 19 tables embedded directly in this HTML so the atlas is fully offline.

It is the second of four sibling atlases (ADR-14):

atlas
scope
audience
Inversion
Phase-2 inversion discovery and per-candidate refinement
Manuscript reviewers; the PI running candidate review
Diversity
θπ, F_ROH, ROH atlas, ancestry-Q stratification, kinship/relatedness
Population-genetics reviewers
Genome
Chromosome-scale assembly stats, synteny, gene tracks, repeats / TE landscape, conserved elements
Genome-assembly reviewers
Population
Samples / QC / families / heterozygosity / diversity / inbreeding (cohort-summary scaffolds)
Breeding-program collaborators

Topic overlap with the Population Atlas is intentional. Both atlases cover heterozygosity / diversity / inbreeding biologically — Population Atlas's pages 4/5/6 are now cohort-summary landing cards with green "→ Diversity Atlas" callouts that take readers straight here for the depth view. Two products, two depths, one source of truth (MODULE_3 supp tables).

← Population Atlas (cohort QC, families, sample manifest) → Inversion Atlas (per-window scrubber, candidate review)

Glossary
12 terms
  • H — genome-wide heterozygosity from the ANGSD folded SFS over the callable mask. Population-diversity estimator equivalent to θπ under the infinite-sites model.
  • θπ — Tajima's π, mean pairwise nucleotide differences per site. Computed in 500 kb windows by ANGSD thetaStat from per-sample SFS.
  • F_ROH — fraction of the callable genome covered by runs of homozygosity ≥ 1 Mb, an ROH-based estimator of inbreeding.
  • F_HOM — H-based inbreeding estimator: 1 − H_i / H̄_pop. Linearly inverse-related to H, so ρ(F_HOM, F_ROH) = +0.704 mirrors ρ(H, F_ROH) = −0.704 — HMM-free cross-validation of ngsF-HMM.
  • ROH bins — short (<1 Mb), medium (1–5 Mb), long (>5 Mb). Long ROH = recent inbreeding; short = ancient.
  • θ in/out ROH — ratio of mean Watterson θ inside vs. outside ROH segments. Typical 0.20–0.30 (4–5× depression).
  • callable genome — 576.9 Mb of mappable, depth-passed positions (mosdepth); F_ROH denominator.
  • K=8 NGSadmix — best-seed assignment to the K=8 solution. Clusters represent hatchery broodline structure.
  • pruned81 — 81 individuals retained after first-degree kinship pruning (NAToRA, θ ≥ 0.177).
  • folded SFS — site-frequency spectrum without ancestral polarisation (no clean outgroup for hatchery cohorts).
  • ngsF-HMM stability — 26/28 chromosomes labelled 'very stable' across 20 random-seed restarts (SZ).
  • broodline — a genealogically distinct hatchery line. K=8 clusters are read as broodline groups.
Cross-references
where to look next
  • Inversion Atlas page 12 (local PCA θπ) — per-window θπ scrubber for hotspot follow-up.
  • Inversion Atlas per-band θπ panel — orthogonal genotype validation for inversion candidates.
  • Population Atlas pages 4 / 5 / 6 — same biological topics at cohort-summary depth.
  • Genome Atlas (planned) — chromosome assembly stats; will overlay these θπ peaks on gene tracks.
  • MS_Inversions_North_african_catfish manuscript — Tables S1–S12 + ST1–ST5 + SD1–SD8 sourced for this atlas.
Roadmap
What's shipped (tabs 1–7) and what's still planned.
v2 — what's shipped
✓ this version
  • All 19 supplementary tables surfaced — S1, S2, S3, S4, S5 (KW + pairwise), S6, S7, S8, S8b, S9, S10, S11, SZ, ST1, ST2, ST3, ST3b, plus methods docs SD1–SD8.
  • 20+ plots: distributions (H, F_ROH, θπ), pairwise scatters (H × F_ROH coloured by K=8, H × longest, θ in/out), per-chromosome bars (θπ ranked, F_ROH ranked, KW −log P, HMM stability ribbon), per-cluster boxes (F_ROH, H), genome-wide hotspot manhattan, per-sample × hotspot heatmap, per-chr × per-sample F_ROH heatmap, ROH length spectrum, stacked F_ROH composition, K-sweep trajectory, Q × H bars.
  • Sortable, filterable, click-to-detail tables: 226-row sample, 28-row per-chr (3 tables), 19-row hotspots, 1,130-row F_ROH bins, 226-row pruning, 28-row HMM stability.

Open questions for the next session
decision points
  1. Should the Diversity Atlas's tab-1 row drill-down link out to the Inversion Atlas's per-sample dashboard (when that exists), or stay self-contained?
  2. Per-sample θπ — run thetaStat on existing SAFs as a one-shot batch, or roll into the next MODULE_3 refresh?
  3. Population Atlas pages 4/5/6 — deprecate, or convert to "summary pages" linking to this atlas? Resolved (this session): pages 4/5/6 became cohort-summary landing cards with green callout buttons that open the corresponding Diversity Atlas tabs.
  4. Tab-4 cluster boxes — add strip-plot of 226 individual points on top of the boxes? Resolved (this session): 226-sample jittered strip plots overlaid on K=8 F_ROH boxes, K=8 H boxes, and Pruning Retained-vs-Removed boxes.
  5. Tab-5 stacked composition — show all 226, or keep top-60 + bottom-60 contrast? Resolved (this session): three-mode toggle — top60+bot60 (default), all-226, top-100 — pill-controlled.
  6. New: kinship heatmap (ngsRelate output) — JSON-ify and surface as Tab 4 panel? Bridges family structure into this atlas where it currently lives only in Population Atlas page 3.
  7. New: per-sample θπ ribbons across all 28 chromosomes — once batch thetaStat completes, surface as Tab 1 click-to-detail expansion?
